entries.definition: a persistent feeling of resentment or ill will towards someone, resulting from a past insult, injury, or wrong
entries.exampleSentence: He's held a grudge against his brother for years over a minor argument at Christmas.

entries.definition: to be unwilling to give, grant, or allow something; to resent having to do something
entries.exampleSentence: I don't grudge him his success — he's worked incredibly hard for it.
